topic.name: Biomass Energy Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures related to the development, financing, and regulation of biomass energy and biorefineries in the United States.

Document 1: Federal Register notice approval process for BLM
Submission timeline for BLM office reviews
Document 2: Amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005
Requires biorefinery recipients to provide financing opportunities
Document 3: Allows 30% energy tax credit for biomass heating property
Repeals exemption from passive activity rules for oil or gas property

topic.name: Medicare Coverage Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at modifying Medicare coverage for specific health services and equipment.

Document 1: provide Medicare coverage for screening computed tomography colonography
colorectal cancer screening test
Document 2: eliminate the 190-day lifetime limit on inpatient psychiatric hospital services
Document 3: restore Medicare treatment of ownership of oxygen equipment
repeals the limitation of Medicare payment to the supplier

topic.name: Education Policy and Reform
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving teacher and principal training, recruitment, and support in the education system.

Document 1: expands loan forgiveness available to public elementary and secondary school teachers
raises to $25,000 the amount of the FFEL or DL outstanding that may be forgiven
Document 2: direct the Secretary of Education to award renewable five-year matching grants to local educational agencies
provide aspiring principals with a pre-service residency for at least one year that focuses on instructional leadership
Document 3: require recognized accrediting agencies to evaluate the quality of distance or correspondence education programs
require those programs to have a secure login and passcode for Internet coursework

topic.name: Tariff Suspension Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the temporary suspension of duties on specific goods and materials in the United States.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on certain structures, parts, and components for use in an isotopic separation facility
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 2: suspend temporarily the duty on certain synthetic staple fibers
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 3: extend the suspension of duty on 1,1,2,2,3,3,4,4,4-Nonafluorobutanesulfonic acid, potassium salt
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States

topic.name: Veterans Affairs Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on employment, health care, and advocacy for veterans.

Document 1: Instructs the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to consider all qualified veterans before any non-veterans for employment
Requires the VA to consider, among qualified veterans, those with a service-connected disability having a rating of 30%
Document 2: Establishes in the Veterans Health Administration (VHA) of the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) an Office of the Ombudsman
serving as a last resort for veterans' health care and benefits complaints and issues that cannot be resolved at a local or regional level
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to reimburse any full-time board-certified health professional
Directs that the expenses incurred be expenses required to maintain licensure

topic.name: Veterans Education and Energy Policy
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislation related to educational benefits for veterans and policies on energy resources and environmental management.

Document 1: Amends the Montgomery GI Bill educational assistance program for veterans
provide educational assistance for veterans discharged under a general discharge
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to modify and set forth provisions concerning tax credits for alternative and renewable energy
Establishes the American-Made Energy Trust Fund
Document 3: amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to direct the Department of the Interior to make available for leasing
conduct lease sales including, at least 50% of the available unleased acreage within each outer Continental Shelf planning area

topic.name: U.S. Immigration Policy
topic.description: Legislation addressing various aspects of immigration laws and regulations in the United States.

Document 1: DREAM Act of 2011 authorizes conditional permanent residency for certain minors.
Requires good moral character and educational achievements.
Document 2: Birthright Citizenship Act of 2013 modifies citizenship criteria for individuals born in the U.S.
Limits citizenship at birth to children of specific categories of parents.
Document 3: CLEAR Act of 2015 empowers state and local law enforcement to enforce immigration laws.
Provides federal support for states that assist in immigration law enforcement.

topic.name: Electromagnetic Pulse Weapons
topic.description: Legislative measures focused on the assessment and accountability of foreign countries' electromagnetic pulse weapons programs.

Document 1: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.
Document 2: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.

topic.name: Wilderness Designation and Management
topic.description: Legislation focused on the designation and management of wilderness areas and federal lands for conservation and recreation purposes.

Document 1: Requires the Secretary of Agriculture to convey specified parcels of federal land to certain counties and cities in central Idaho.
Designates specified parcels of federal land in central Idaho as wilderness areas.
Document 2: Amends the Oregon Wilderness Act of 1984 to designate certain lands in Siskiyou National Forest as the 'Copper Salmon Wilderness.'
Designates certain National Forest System land in Oregon as a potential wilderness area for eventual inclusion in the Copper Salmon Wilderness.
Document 3: Designates certain lands in Rocky Mountain National Park, Colorado, as the Rocky Mountain National Park Wilderness.
Provides for the inclusion of specified potential wilderness land within the Park in such Wilderness.

topic.name: Child and Elder Protection Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at protecting vulnerable populations, specifically children and the elderly, from exploitation, abuse, and discrimination in various social services.

Document 1: prohibit federal payments for foster care and adoption assistance to a state or local government that discriminates against any entity
provides adoption or foster care services to only those couples who are united in marriage
Document 2: study and report to Congress on state laws and practices relating to elder abuse, neglect, and exploitation
award victim advocacy grants for study of the special needs of victims of elder abuse, neglect, and exploitation
Document 3: expand the reporting requirements of electronic communication and remote computing service providers with respect to violations of child sexual exploitation and pornography laws
requires service providers to preserve images of child pornography for evidentiary purposes

topic.name: Mental Health and Homelessness Services
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving mental health care access and support for individuals experiencing homelessness and veterans with combat-related mental health issues.

Document 1: authorize the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to award matching grants to states or Indian tribes
construction or modernization of facilities used to provide community-based mental health and substance abuse services
Document 2: require the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration to design national strategies
Priority for grants is given to applicants that target services to individuals and families who have experienced more homelessness
Document 3: veterans who experience combat-related mental health wounds should have immediate and consistent access to comprehensive mental health care
establish standards and procedures to ensure that a veteran who participated in a classified mission or served in a sensitive unit may access VA mental health care

topic.name: Pharmaceutical and Opioid Regulation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the regulation, approval, and safe use of pharmaceuticals, particularly related to opioids.

Document 1: require the Food and Drug Administration to facilitate the development and expedite the review of a new drug, biological product, or device
amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act
Document 2: direct the Administrator of the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration to award grants to states and nonprofit community organizations to distribute culturally sensitive educational materials about the dangers of opioid abuse
establish a Controlled Substances Clinical Standards Commission to develop safe dosing standards for all forms of methadone
Document 3: amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act
make clerical corrections to provisions of such Act

topic.name: Water Resource Management
topic.description: The documents focus on federal initiatives related to the planning, design, and implementation of water resource management and ecosystem restoration projects.

Document 1: Restoring the Everglades, an American Legacy Act of 2007 - Authorizes the Secretary of the Army to carry out the project for ecosystem restoration, water supply, flood control, and protection of water quality.
Authorizes the Secretary of the Army to carry out the project for ecosystem restoration, Picayune Strand, Collier County, Florida.
Document 2: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior to participate in the design, planning, and construction of an advanced water treatment plant facility.
Authorize the Secretary of the Interior to participate in the design, planning, and construction of a project to expand reclaimed water distribution, storage, and treatment facilities.
Document 3: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior to participate in the planning, design, and construction of water recycling facilities.
Enhance and restore riparian habitat in the Black Wash Sonoran Desert ecosystem.

topic.name: Mortgage Regulation and Assistance
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at regulating mortgage origination practices and providing assistance to homeowners to prevent foreclosures and manage mortgage-related issues.

Document 1: Amends the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act of 2008 with respect to mortgages acquired by the Secretary of the Treasury.
Requires federal property managers to minimize foreclosures and implement loan modifications.
Document 2: Amends the Truth in Lending Act with respect to the prohibitions against steering incentives in connection with residential mortgage loan origination.
Declares that mortgage originators can compensate other employees based on the principal amount of the loan.
Document 3: Limits the funds that may be provided to government-sponsored enterprises as part of the Amended and Restated Senior Preferred Stock Purchase Agreement.

topic.name: Coral Reef Conservation Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at the conservation, research, and restoration of coral reef ecosystems in the United States.

Document 1: amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000
directs NOAA to submit to Congress a national coral reef action strategy
Document 2: amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act
promote coral reef research and conservation
Document 3: extends the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds
allows the Coral Reef Conservation Fund to be used to address emergency response actions

topic.name: Rural Education and Agriculture Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing educational opportunities and agricultural support for rural communities.

Document 1: Amends part B of the Rural Education Initiative to revise the Small, Rural School Achievement program
Limits eligibility to rural local educational agencies for federal formula grants
Document 2: Amends the Food Security Act to extend assistance to disadvantaged or beginning farmers
Provides priority assistance for new farming opportunities, including young and socially disadvantaged farmers
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to award competitive grants to reduce processed food in school programs
Gives grant priority to areas with high rates of students eligible for free or reduced price meals

topic.name: Renewable Energy Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislation related to renewable energy standards and incentives for electric vehicles.

Document 1: repeal the Environmental Protection Agency's (EPA) renewable fuel program
Document 2: tax credits for the purchase of a new qualified plug-in electric drive motor vehicle
rebates paid by electric utilities to purchasers of or converters to plug-in electric drive vehicles
Document 3: make it unlawful for any person to sell gasoline with less than 10% renewable fuel by volume

topic.name: Data Privacy and Security Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the protection of personally identifiable information and the establishment of protocols for data breaches across various sectors.

Document 1: Defines 'sensitive personally identifiable information' including various data elements.
Imposes penalties for concealment of security breaches that cause economic harm.
Document 2: Requires timely notification to individuals whose sensitive personal information could be compromised.
Mandates federal agency information security programs to include data breach notification procedures.
Document 3: Amends FERPA to require educational agencies to establish information security practices.
Expands definition of 'education records' to include electronically maintained records.

topic.name: Fraud Prevention and Protection
topic.description: Legislation aimed at preventing fraud and protecting vulnerable populations, particularly seniors, from financial exploitation and identity theft.

Document 1: disseminate to seniors and their families and caregivers information on mail, telemarketing, and Internet fraud targeting seniors
establish and administer a competitive grant program for mail, telemarketing, and Internet fraud prevention education programs for senior citizens
Document 2: prohibits any federal officer or employee from hiring any Internal Revenue Service (IRS) employees until the IRS submits to Congress
a written certification that the IRS does not employ any individual who has a seriously delinquent tax debt
Document 3: provide individuals who have been affected by a breach of personnel records in a database network with complimentary identity protection coverage
establish a task force to develop specific recommendations on mitigating adverse consequences relating to data breaches

topic.name: Financial Regulatory Reforms
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislative amendments and acts aimed at reforming the financial regulatory framework in the United States to enhance stability and protect stakeholders.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act and the Federal Credit Union Act to declare that certain federal agencies shall not be deemed to have waived any confidentiality applicable to information
Shields also from the waiver of confidentiality any information submitted by a person to either a federal banking agency.
Document 2: This bill amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to declare that any action of the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) that requires a bank holding company to provide funds or other assets to a subsidiary depository institution is neither effective nor enforceable
The Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act is amended, with respect to systemic risk determination and the treatment of insurance companies.
Document 3: Establishes an independent Financial Stability Council to review, and approve or prohibit issuance of, or require modification of, any rule or regulation issued by any federal financial regulator
Grants Council actions precedence in any conflict with any regulatory action of a federal financial regulator.

topic.name: Endangered Species Protection
topic.description: Legislation and regulations concerning the protection and listing of endangered and threatened species under the Endangered Species Act.

Document 1: Prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from offering for oil and gas leasing in the Lease Sale 193 Area before the listing of the polar bear as a threatened or endangered species.
determination regarding its critical habitat
Document 2: Prohibits the Department of the Interior from using any funds to make a determination on the listing of the northern long-eared bat under the Endangered Species Act.
Interior proposed in October 2013 to list the northern long-eared bat as endangered.
Document 3: Amends the Endangered Species Act of 1973 to require the Secretary of the Interior to use empirical data for determining species inclusion on the endangered or threatened species list.
Requires the Secretary to notify and provide a copy of a petition to the agency of each state in which the species is believed to occur.

topic.name: Federal Legislative Actions
topic.description: This cluster discusses various federal legislative measures and designations related to government buildings, transportation regulations, and national symbols.

Document 1: Redesignates the federal building and courthouse located at 200 East Wall Street in Midland, Texas, known as the George Mahon Federal Building, as the 'George H.W. Bush and George W. Bush United States Courthouse and George Mahon Federal Building.'
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) to implement or enforce a requirement providing for the screening, testing, or treatment of airmen or air traffic controllers for sleep disorders.
Document 3: This bill adopts the North American bison as the national mammal of the United States.

topic.name: Transportation Infrastructure Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the improvement and safety of transportation infrastructure, specifically focusing on rail and highway projects.

Document 1: Authorizes the Altamont Corridor Rail Project for final design and construction
Allows for the use of any alternative analysis or environmental impact report relating the Project
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Transportation to modify certain federal regulations
Revises requirements for the highway safety improvement program to count installation, replacement, and upgrade of highway signs and pavement markings
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Transportation to establish a program to eliminate duplicative environmental reviews
Authorizes a state to use state environmental review and approval laws and procedures in lieu of federal environmental laws

topic.name: Nanotechnology and Innovation Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at promoting advancements in nanotechnology and innovation through funding, research, and development initiatives.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Commerce... to establish such a Partnership to provide funding for precommercial nanomanufacturing research and development projects.
Establishes a Nanotechnology Startup Advisory Council.
Document 2: Directs the President to convene a National Science and Technology Summit to examine the health and direction of the United States' science, technology, engineering, and mathematics enterprises.

Requires the Director of the Office of Science and Technology Policy to contract with the National Academy of Sciences to conduct and complete a study to identify, and review methods to mitigate, new forms of risk for businesses beyond conventional operational and financial risk.

topic.name: Health Care Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on modifying health care policies, insurance coverage, and tax regulations related to medical expenses.

Document 1: Repeals provisions of the Internal Revenue Code that limit payments for medications from health savings accounts
allowing distributions from such accounts for over-the-counter drugs.
Document 2: Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act to prohibit a group health plan from denying benefits for treatment of injuries from recreational activities.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow tax-free rollovers from retirement plans into individual retirement accounts.

topic.name: Small Business Assistance Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing support and resources for small businesses through various programs and initiatives.

Document 1: reauthorize for FY2011-FY2015 the national small business tree planting program
SBA makes grants to states for contracting with small businesses
Document 2: amends the Small Business Act with respect to the authority of the Small Business Administration
provide grants, financial assistance, loans, export assistance, and subcontracting opportunities
Document 3: raises the governmentwide prime contract award goal for participation by small business concerns
requires each agency head to consult with the Administrator of the Small Business Administration

topic.name: Indian Tribal Governance and Resources
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at enhancing the governance, economic development, and resource management for Indian tribes in the United States.

Document 1: Repeals a restriction on treating as Indian country certain lands held in trust for Indian pueblos
Prohibits gaming on such land.
Document 2: Establishes the Indian Irrigation Fund
Interior shall establish a program to address the deferred maintenance needs of Indian irrigation projects.
Document 3: Amends the Native American Housing Assistance and Self-Determination Act
Allows Indian housing block grant recipients to use income that is realized from program income without restriction.

topic.name: Health Care and Social Security Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to health care programs and benefits under the Social Security Act.

Document 1: increase a premium, deductible, copayment, or other charge for health care provided under TRICARE
rates of monthly basic pay for members of the Armed Forces are increased
Document 2: provide to each eligible individual annually a statement of Medicare part A contributions and benefits
coordination with the annual mailing of Social Security account statements
Document 3: Amends title II of the Social Security Act
last payment of the monthly benefit for that month shall be half the usual benefit amount

topic.name: Regulatory Reforms and Budget Management
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at reforming regulatory processes and managing federal budgetary practices.

Document 1: Requires federal agencies to report on regulatory costs and benefits
Establishes procedures for approval of significant guidance documents
Document 2: Amends the Congressional Budget Act to include a benchmark plan to reduce discretionary spending
Makes it out of order to consider budget resolutions that violate this Act
Document 3: Prohibits the EPA from lowering air quality standards until certain conditions are met
Requires direct air quality monitoring for nonattainment areas

topic.name: U.S. Policies on Iran
topic.description: Documents discussing various U.S. legislative measures aimed at addressing Iran's nuclear capabilities and related international security concerns.

Document 1: Support for diplomatic efforts relating to preventing Iran from acquiring nuclear weapons
States that nothing in this Act shall be construed as authorizing the use of force against Iran.
Document 2: Authorizes the Department of Defense to provide assistance to foreign countries to interdict shipments contravening U.N. Security Council Resolutions
Interdiction training and logistical support for seizure of items related to the Iranian nuclear issue.
Document 3: Prohibits the use of funds by the United States to make a payment to the government of Iran
Until the President certifies that Iran has paid all compensatory damages awarded to U.S. persons.

topic.name: Housing and Education Support for Vulnerable Youth
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving access to housing and educational resources for homeless and foster youth.

Document 1: amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to revise provisions related to financial aid and educational programs for children and youths who are homeless or in foster care.
expands the duties of the Student Loan Ombudsman to include the review and resolution of complaints regarding such determinations.
Document 2: Amends the United States Housing Act of 1937 with respect to annual review of low-income families' income for eligibility requirements for certain federal assisted housing programs.
Requires PHAs to also review a family's income receiving Section 8 (rental assistance voucher program) at least once every three years.
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to make competitive grants to local governments, public housing agencies (PHAs), or nonprofit entities owning a major housing project to implement transformational programs in eligible neighborhoods with a concentration of extreme poverty and severely distressed housing.
Prescribes requirements for PHAs that utilize housing tax credits under the Internal Revenue Code for rental housing units.

topic.name: Campaign Finance Reform
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating the funding and expenditures of political campaigns in order to enhance transparency and limit financial influence in elections.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to require the identification of funding and authorizing sources
Requires individual disclosure statements about the head of the entity paying for the communication
Document 2: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to limit expenditures by candidates for the House of Representatives
Provides for indexing such amounts for inflation
Document 3: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to eliminate the limitations on direct contributions to candidates in federal elections
Require 24-hour notification to the Secretary of the Senate for all direct contributions to candidates in excess of $200

topic.name: Consumer Protection Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at protecting consumers in financial transactions and education from unfair practices.

Document 1: prohibit a debt collector from bringing, or threatening to bring, legal action against any consumer on a debt in which the statute of limitations has expired
Requires a debt collector... to disclose to the consumer that: (1) the debt has been transferred to the debt collector
Document 2: Amends the Electronic Fund Transfer Act to authorize the issuance of remotely created checks only if the consumer has designated in writing a person to issue them
Empowers the CFPB to investigate violations of this Act
Document 3: Makes any institution of higher education... ineligible to participate in a federal financial assistance program, unless it meets specified student consumer protection requirements
Requires each program to: (1) fully prepare students to satisfy those entry pre-conditions

topic.name: Emergency Communications and Access
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at ensuring access to communication services during emergencies and enhancing the operational capabilities of wireless communications systems.

Document 1: voluntary policies outlined in the Wireless Network Resiliency Cooperative Framework should be adhered to
access to communication services during emergencies
Document 2: expand eligibility for specified licenses authorizing the operation of low power auxiliary stations
protect wireless microphone operations from interference
Document 3: establish a broadband lifeline program enabling qualifying low-income customers to purchase broadband service at reduced charges

topic.name: Veterans Affairs Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving benefits and support for military personnel and their families.

Document 1: Amends the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 1998 to make eligible under a Department of Defense (DOD) survivor annuity program...
those surviving spouses who: (1) remarried after age 55; or (2) remarried at any age, but whose subsequent marriage was terminated due to death, annulment, or divorce.
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of Defense to carry out programs under which commissioned officers of the nurse corps serve as full-time faculty of accredited schools of nursing...
receive scholarships leading to a graduate degree in nursing in exchange for service as nursing school faculty...
Document 3: Shortens the length of the obligated period of active duty service required of certain members of the Armed Forces to receive certain veterans' education benefits.

topic.name: Consumer Product Safety Regulations
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing safety standards for consumer products to prevent health hazards.

Document 1: Directs the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) to publish the American National Standard for Single and Multiple Station Carbon Monoxide Alarms as mandatory consumer product safety standards.
Requires the CPSC to establish a grant program to provide assistance to states and local governments that require approved carbon monoxide alarms.
Document 2: Requires any mobile phone containing a digital camera to sound a tone whenever a photograph is taken.
Treats the requirement as a consumer product safety standard and requires enforcement by the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC).
Document 3: Prohibits the distribution of a food if its container is composed, in whole or in part, of bisphenol A (BPA).
Sets forth labeling requirements for a product granted a waiver.

topic.name: Labor Rights and Protections
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing labor rights, protecting employees from discrimination, and ensuring transparency and accountability within labor organizations.

Document 1: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require certification of a bargaining representative without an election if a majority of employees authorize it.
Sets forth special procedural requirements for reaching an initial collective bargaining agreement.
Document 2: Prohibits any employment discrimination or acts of reprisal against persons with service-related injuries or disabilities.
Document 3: Amends the Labor-Management Reporting and Disclosure Act to impose penalties on labor organizations for failing to provide members with required information.
Authorizes the Secretary of Labor to bring actions for enforcement of reporting requirements.

topic.name: Immigration Detention Policies
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and policies related to the detention and treatment of individuals, particularly in the context of immigration enforcement and the rights of detainees and their families.

Document 1: Prohibits federal funds from being used to transfer any individual detained by the United States at Naval Station Guantanamo Bay, Cuba.
house any such individual at a Florida installation or detention center.
Document 2: Sets forth apprehension procedures for immigration enforcement-related activities engaged in by the Department of Homeland Security (DHS).
requiring that the interests of children be considered in decisions regarding detainee release, detention, or transfer.
Document 3: the detention facility at Naval Station, Guantanamo Bay, Cuba, shall remain in use indefinitely by the United States as a detention facility.
any individual who is detained at Naval Station, Guantanamo Bay, Cuba, may only be tried by military commission.

topic.name: Education Policy and Grants
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to education improvement, environmental literacy, and anti-harassment measures in educational institutions.

Document 1: No Child Left Inside Act of 2007 - Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to require states...
Authorizes the Secretary to award competitive matching grants to nonprofit organizations, states, LEAs, or IHEs for activities to improve and support environmental education...
Document 2: Literacy Education for All, Results for the Nation Act or LEARN Act - Authorizes the Secretary of Education to award grants to states for comprehensive planning to improve the literacy of children...
Gives subgrant priority to applicants serving a high number or percentage of impoverished children...
Document 3: Tyler Clementi Higher Education Anti-Harassment Act of 2010 - Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to require each institution of higher education to include in its annual security report a statement of policy regarding harassment...
Authorizes the Secretary of Education to award competitive grants to IHEs to initiate, expand, or improve programs to: (1) prevent the harassment of students...

topic.name: Legislative Reforms for Governance
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at improving democratic processes, economic growth, and electoral integrity in the United States.

Document 1: Democracy for Dairy Producers Act of 2007 requires notice of referendum issues and voting procedures for milk producers.
It mandates adjustments to reflect individual producer votes.
Document 2: Job Creation and Economic Growth Act amends the Budget Control Act to focus on job creation and economic growth.
It requires recommendations for improving U.S. employment levels.
Document 3: Vote Tabulation Audit Act of 2008 sets standards for transparent vote tabulation in federal elections.
It mandates hand recounts in cases of vote discrepancies.

topic.name: Firearm Legislation and Self-Defense
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts related to firearm rights, self-defense provisions, and regulations governing the use of firearms in specific contexts.

Document 1: Protect Law Enforcement Armor Act expands the definition of 'armor piercing ammunition' and regulates its use.
Prohibits the manufacture, sale, possession, or transfer of certain handguns using armor piercing ammunition.
Document 2: Citizens' Self-Defense Act declares the right to obtain firearms for self-defense against perceived threats.
Authorizes legal action for violations of rights under this Act.
Document 3: Recreational Lands Self-Defense Act prohibits regulations against firearm possession at specific water resources projects.
Ensures compliance with state laws regarding firearm possession.

topic.name: Aviation Customer Service Regulations
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving customer service and operational standards in the airline industry.

Document 1: extend funding for airport planning and development
limit the liability of air carriers to third parties for claims arising out of acts of terrorism
Document 2: require commercial air carriers to allow each air passenger to check one bag
imposes a civil penalty on air carriers that fail to comply
Document 3: require a passenger air carrier to publish customer service data
disclose on-time performance and cancellation rate for flights

topic.name: Food Security and Nutrition Policies
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving food security, nutrition education, and support for food production systems.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to award grants to establish gardens that are fully operated by veterans and are designed to produce food.
Gives priority to entities seeking to establish a garden located on, or convenient to, the grounds of a Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) medical facility.
Document 2: Declares that it shall be U.S. policy to reduce global hunger, advance nutrition, increase food security.
Establishes in the Executive Office of the President a White House Office on Global Hunger and Food Security.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to authorize a food service establishment to provide nutrition information.
Requires the labeling of a standard food item served or offered for sale in a food service establishment.

topic.name: Native Hawaiian Rights and Services
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the rights, governance, and health services for Native Hawaiians.

Document 1: the United States has a special political and legal relationship with Native Hawaiians
Native Hawaiians have the right to autonomy in their internal affairs, to self-determination and self-governance
Document 2: provide full reimbursement for medical assistance given to a Native Hawaiian through a federally-qualified health center
Native Hawaiian health care system
Document 3: an institution determined to have enrolled a substantial number of minority, low-income students who received assistance under the Higher Education Act of 1965
Native Hawaiian-serving institution

topic.name: Servicemember Financial Protections
topic.description: Legislation aimed at safeguarding the financial rights and obligations of servicemembers and their families during military service.

Document 1: Amends the Servicemembers Civil Relief Act
Increases penalties for consumer credit reporting violations involving servicemembers deployed to an overseas combat zone.
Document 2: Prohibits the sale, foreclosure, or seizure of the subject property for the service period
Increases the civil penalties for violations of the sale or foreclosure prohibitions.

topic.name: U.S. Postal Service Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the operations, funding, and environmental initiatives of the U.S. Postal Service.

Document 1: Directs the Office of Personnel Management (OPM) to make a determination or redetermination of postal surplus or supplemental liability.
Authorizes arbitration boards to consider the financial condition of the USPS in rendering decisions.
Document 2: Amends the National Energy Conservation Policy Act to expand the definition of 'energy or water conservation measure' under such Act to include, in the case of a contract in which the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) is a party.
Directs the Postmaster General to develop guidelines for USPS vehicles that provide for specified carbon dioxide emissions and fuel economy standards.
Document 3: Directs the U.S. Postal Service to issue a semipostal to benefit the Peace Corps.

topic.name: Public Safety and Education Funding
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing public safety and improving educational infrastructure through targeted funding and grant programs.

Document 1: Requires the Secretary of Education to make grants to states for the modernization, renovation, or repair of public schools
Allocates grant funds among states on the basis of the relative portion of school improvement funds provided to local educational agencies (LEAs)
Document 2: Amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 to waive the grantee matching funds requirement
for purchasing bulletproof armor vests to the extent that such vests are purchased for law enforcement officers who work in high security risk areas

topic.name: Abortion Rights and Protections
topic.description: Legislation focused on protecting healthcare providers' rights to refuse participation in abortion-related activities without facing discrimination or penalties.

Document 1: prohibition against the federal government and state and local governments penalizing or discriminating against a health care provider based on the provider's refusal to be involved in, or provide coverage for, abortion
investigate complaints alleging discrimination based on an individual's religious belief, moral conviction, or refusal to be involved in an abortion.
Document 2: prohibit the provision of federal education funding to state or local educational agencies that make health services available to students through school-based health centers unless those centers certify that they will not provide students with abortions
certification requirements for school-based health centers regarding abortion services.
Document 3: codify the prohibition against any action by the federal government and any state or local government receiving federal financial assistance to subject a health professional to discrimination on the basis that the entity refuses to participate in abortion-related activities
creates a cause of action for any violation of the abortion discrimination prohibition.

topic.name: Emergency Health and Safety Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving emergency health responses and safety measures in educational and medical settings.

Document 3: requires IHEs to notify students about fire safety systems
expands information an IHE must submit in its annual fire safety report

topic.name: Military Sexual Assault Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the prevention, reporting, and forensic examination of sexual assault within the military and associated legal frameworks.

Document 1: Requires sexual assault forensic examiners (SAFEs) for the Armed Forces to be members of the Armed Forces and civilian personnel of the Department of Defense (DOD) or the Department of Homeland Security (DHS).
Requires at least one SAFE to be assigned to each naval vessel.
Document 2: Amends the Uniform Code of Military Justice (UCMJ) to state that an accused may retain private counsel before a general court-martial or special court-martial.
Will be reimbursed for such counsel if the accused is acquitted or the charges are dismissed or withdrawn.
Document 3: Amends the DNA Analysis Backlog Elimination Act of 2000 to authorize the Attorney General to make Debbie Smith grants under such Act to states or local governments.
Ensure that the collection and processing of DNA evidence by law enforcement agencies from crimes is carried out in an appropriate and timely manner.

topic.name: District of Columbia Governance
topic.description: Legislation related to the governance and voting rights of residents in the District of Columbia.

Document 1: Restores the right of District of Columbia residents to participate as Maryland residents in congressional elections and presidential elections.
Repeals provisions of the District of Columbia Delegate Act.
Document 2: Establishes the Office of the District Attorney for the District of Columbia.
Requires the DA to be elected to a four-year term on a partisan basis.
Document 3: Restores the right of District of Columbia residents to participate as Maryland residents in congressional elections and presidential elections.
Repeals provisions of the District of Columbia Delegate Act.

topic.name: U.S. Trade Enforcement and Manufacturing Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing U.S. trade enforcement mechanisms and supporting domestic manufacturing through grants and regulations.

Document 1: Authorizes the Secretary of Commerce to establish and carry out a program to award grants to any entity that is a manufacturer in the United States.
Defines a 'nonavailable item' as an article that... is not mined, produced, or manufactured in the United States in sufficient and reasonably available commercial quantities.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to establish within the Office of International Trade of the U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) a Trade Remedy Law Enforcement Division.
Requires the Group also to use information available from the Automated Targeting System... to administer such methodologies and standards.

topic.name: HIV/AIDS and Public Health Initiatives
topic.description: This cluster addresses legislative efforts and programs aimed at combating HIV/AIDS, particularly within vulnerable communities, and improving public health responses.

Document 1: Establishes within the Executive Office of the President the position of Iraqi Displacement Coordinator who shall identify programs that address the humanitarian needs of displaced Iraqis
encourage the government of Iraq to develop a long-term plan for the voluntary and safe return of displaced Iraqis to their homes.
Document 2: Authorize the Director of the Office of Minority Health of the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to make grants to public health agencies and faith-based organizations to conduct HIV/AIDS prevention, testing, and related outreach activities
expand and intensify HIV/AIDS activities in that community.
Document 3: Establishes the Prize Fund for HIV/AIDS
Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to designate a Prize Fund Director to administer the Fund.

topic.name: Education and Family Support
topic.description: Legislative efforts focused on improving education quality and supporting military families through parental leave.

Document 1: ensure that all students have equitable access to a high-quality, well-rounded education
promote programs that encourage parental and community involvement
Document 2: a member of the armed forces, regardless of gender or marital status, shall be authorized to take at least 84 days of parental leave
both service members shall be authorized to take parental leave
Document 3: reprisal (discharge, demotion, or other discriminatory acts) is prohibited for disclosures made by a DOD contract employee

topic.name: Financial Legislation and Tax Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures related to financial regulations, tax exemptions, and monetary policy aimed at enhancing economic stability and support for education financing.

Document 1: amends the Internal Revenue Code
expand the operations of such corporations
Document 2: amends the Internal Revenue Code
exempts such an entity from treatment as a bank
Document 3: amends the Federal Reserve Act
establish metrics to evaluate whether long-term price stability is being achieved

topic.name: National Security and Civil Rights
topic.description: Documents address the intersection of national security, law enforcement, and civil rights protections.

Document 1: Establishes a Committee on National Security Personnel within the Executive Office of the President.
Requires the Committee to issue a National Security Human Capital Strategy.
Document 2: Adopts the definition of 'hate crime' as set forth in the Violent Crime Control and Law Enforcement Act of 1994.
Authorizes the Attorney General to provide technical, forensic, prosecutorial, or other assistance in the criminal investigation or prosecution of a violent crime, a hate crime.
Document 3: Amends the Civil Rights Act of 1964 to declare that discrimination based on disparate impact under federally assisted programs is established only if a person aggrieved by discrimination demonstrates that a covered entity has a policy causing a disparate impact.
Authorizes an aggrieved person to bring a civil action in federal or state court to enforce such person's rights.

topic.name: Commemorative and Environmental Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on commemorating historical figures and managing environmental resources.

Document 1: Establishes the Ronald Reagan Centennial Commission to honor Ronald Reagan on the occasion of the 100th anniversary of his birth.
Prohibits the obligation of federal funds to carry out this Act.
Document 2: Directs the President to establish the Hudson-Mohawk River Basin Commission to coordinate activities associated with the management and use of resources in the Hudson-Mohawk Watershed.
Requires the Commission to develop and implement plans, policies, and projects relating to the Basin's water resources.
Document 3: Authorizes the World War I Memorial Foundation to establish a commemorative work rededicating the District of Columbia War Memorial as a National and District of Columbia World War I Memorial.

topic.name: Transportation Funding and Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the financing and regulation of transportation systems, including aviation, automotive fuel, and infrastructure.

Document 1: impose an excise tax on aviation-grade kerosene
dedicates revenues from the taxes imposed on aviation gasoline and aviation-grade kerosene to carry out air traffic control modernization.
Document 2: repeal the excise tax on medical device manufacturers
amends the Internal Revenue Code
Document 3: requires each manufacturer to ensure that at least 50% of 2011 and 2012 model year automobiles are dual fueled
provides for infrastructure installation subgrants to direct retailers of ethanol fuel blends

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Policy in Asia
topic.description: This cluster discusses U.S. legislative measures aimed at promoting human rights and defense relations in Vietnam and Taiwan.

Document 1: Prohibits U.S. nonhumanitarian assistance to the government of Vietnam in excess of FY2012 amounts unless the President certifies to Congress that the government of Vietnam has made substantial progress respecting political, media, and religious freedoms.
Expresses the sense of Congress that Vietnam should be designated as a country of particular concern for religious freedom.
Document 2: States that it shall be U.S. policy to support Taiwan and the human rights of its people.
Authorizes the President to accept a letter of request from Taiwan for price and availability data regarding the F-16C/D Fighting Falcon.

topic.name: Public Health Preparedness
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing public health systems and responses to health emergencies and training in preventive medicine.

Document 1: authorizes the Secretary of Health and Human Services to award grants for training in preventive medicine specialties
requires grant funds for residency programs and curriculum development
Document 2: authorizes the Secretary of Health and Human Services to sell pediatric vaccines to public health departments
requires manufacturers to notify the Secretary 12 months prior to discontinuing vaccine production
Document 3: authorizes funds for Zika virus response and preparedness

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Acts on Governance and Defense
topic.description: This cluster includes legislative acts related to governance, public information access, and defense partnerships involving the United States.

Document 1: amends the Internal Revenue Code to modify the income source rules involving U.S. possessions
specifies that the income is attributable to an office or fixed place of business within the United States.
Document 2: establishes a Public Online Information Advisory Committee to coordinate and encourage the government's efforts to make information available
requires public records to be permanently available on the Internet.
Document 3: expresses the sense of Congress that the U.S.-India defense partnership is vital to regional and international stability
amends the Arms Export Control Act to extend special foreign military sales status to India.

topic.name: Workplace Safety and Health Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing workplace safety, health standards, and employee protections across various sectors.

Document 1: Amends the Occupational Safety and Health Act of 1970 (OSHA) to expand its coverage to federal, state, and local government employees.
Sets forth increased protections for whistleblowers under OSHA.
Document 2: Revises specified administrative requirements for the National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB).
Authorizes the NTSB by subpoena to summon witnesses and obtain any and all evidence relevant to an accident investigation.
Document 3: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require hospitals to implement staffing plans that meet specified ratios for direct care registered nurse-to-patient staffing levels.
Prohibits retaliation by hospitals for reporting violations of staffing requirements.

topic.name: Workforce Development and Assistance
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at supporting unemployed individuals through educational and financial assistance programs.

Document 1: Amends the Workforce Investment Act of 1988 to require the Secretary of Labor to establish the Unemployment Tuition Assistance Program
tuition assistance available to individuals who became or become unemployed due to the recession for their pursuit of education and job training
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exclude from the definition of full-time employee any individual who is a long-term unemployed individual
Authorizes the Secretary to issue employment relocation loans of up to $10,000 to long-term unemployed individuals

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Acts on Regulation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislative acts that regulate various sectors including foreign aid, gambling, and state-specific exemptions.

Document 1: Directs the President to establish guidelines for the establishment of measurable goals, performance metrics, and monitoring and evaluation plans for U.S. foreign assistance.
Requires such guidelines to provide direction to federal departments and agencies that administer U.S. foreign assistance.
Document 2: Amends the federal criminal code to provide that criminal penalties under such code and other provisions of federal law for transmitting bets and wages on sporting events or contests shall not apply to any bet or wager that is permissible under the Interstate Horseracing Act of 1978.
Document 3: Amends the federal judicial code to exempt a lottery, sweepstakes, or other betting, gambling, or waging scheme operating exclusively in New Jersey from the prohibition against a governmental entity sponsoring, operating, advertising, or promoting sports gambling.

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Policy Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative measures related to U.S. foreign policy, particularly regarding crisis response, assessments of conflict regions, and designations of foreign entities as terrorist organizations.

Document 1: Amends the State Department Basic Authorities Act of 1956 to extend Department of State authority
Eliminates the requirement that such authority be used to facilitate assignment to Afghanistan or Iraq
Document 2: Establishes in the legislative branch the Afghanistan-Pakistan Study Group
Conduct an independent assessment of the situation in Afghanistan and Pakistan
Document 3: Expresses the sense of Congress that Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) meets the criteria for designation as a foreign terrorist organization
The Secretary shall report to Congress on whether the IRGC meets such criteria

topic.name: U.S. Trade and Travel Policies
topic.description: Legislative measures regarding the regulation of trade and travel between the U.S. and Cuba, as well as broader energy export policies.

Document 1: Prohibits the President from regulating or prohibiting travel to or from Cuba by U.S. citizens or legal residents
any regulation restricting or prohibiting such travel shall have no effect
Document 2: Amends the Energy Policy and Conservation Act to repeal the authority of the President to restrict exports of coal, petroleum products, natural gas
Directs the Bureau of Industry and Security of the Department of Commerce to grant licenses to export crude oil to a country unless...
Document 3: Amends the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 to repeal the embargo on trade with Cuba
Authorizes the President to impose export controls with respect to Cuba and exercise certain authorities under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act

topic.name: Federal Procurement and Identity Verification
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to federal procurement policies, identity verification for banking, and consular services, highlighting the intersection of government operations and financial regulations.

Document 1: Requires the Administrator for Federal Procurement Policy to issue guidance to federal agencies for reinvigorating the role of the competition advocate
agency chief information officers to use transparency mechanisms to report to the Office of Management and Budget (OMB)
Document 2: Authorizes a financial institution, when verifying the identity of a non-U.S. citizen or national who is seeking to open a bank account, to accept only: (1) a Social Security number, (2) a passport number and the country of issuance
Document 3: Amends the Department of State and Related Agency Appropriations Act, 2005 to authorize the Secretary of State to charge consular services fees

topic.name: Environmental Legislation and Policy
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislative measures aimed at promoting environmental protection, resource management, and energy security.

Document 1: Instructs the President to promulgate regulations to ensure that aviation fuel, motor vehicle fuel, home heating oil, and boiler fuel sold or introduced into commerce in the United States contains prescribed volumes of coal-derived fuel.
Sets forth standards for reduction of greenhouse gas emissions levels.
Document 2: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior to participate in the design, planning, and construction of recycled water projects.
Expresses the intent of Congress that a comprehensive water recycling program for the San Francisco Bay Area include the San Jose Area water reclamation and reuse program.
Document 3: Encourages the partial or complete remediation of inactive and abandoned mine sites for the public good by good samaritans.
Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to authorize a permitting authority to issue good samaritan discharge permits.

topic.name: Health and Safety Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving public health and safety standards related to occupational exposure and health claims.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to allow food producers to make a disease or health-related claim about a food based on legitimate scientific research.
Prohibits the Secretary of Health and Human Services from restricting the distribution of information that is not false or misleading.
Document 2: Amends the Energy Employees Occupational Illness Compensation Program Act to include persons employed at the Rocky Flats site for compensation related to occupational illness.
Defines a 'member of the Special Exposure Cohort' for health benefits.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Mine Safety and Health Act to transfer the duty to establish a schedule reducing respirable dust concentration to the Secretary of Labor.
Directs the Secretary to issue regulations lowering permissible exposure levels to prevent respiratory diseases in miners.

topic.name: Volunteer Liability Protection
topic.description: Legislation aimed at providing liability protection for volunteers in various nonprofit activities.

Document 1: exempt from liability a nonprofit volunteer pilot organization
flies for public benefit
Document 2: extend by 14 months the Child Safety Pilot Program
obtain national and state criminal history background checks on their volunteers
Document 3: deem a health professional volunteer providing primary health care
liability protection for volunteers in community health centers

topic.name: Legislative Amendments and Transparency
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at amending existing laws to enhance transparency and accountability in various sectors, including education and legal claims.

Document 1: This bill amends federal bankruptcy law concerning a trust formed under a reorganization plan following the discharge in bankruptcy of a debtor corporation
Such a trust shall file with the bankruptcy court quarterly reports, available on the public docket.
Document 2: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to require the Secretary of Education to award formula grants to states
Requires LEAs that receive allocations to provide written notice to the parent of each child who is taught for two or more consecutive weeks by a substitute teacher.

topic.name: Federal Budget and Tax Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to federal funding limitations, tax regulations, and incentives aimed at domestic job creation and compliance in higher education.

Document 1: Limits funding for federal fleet vehicle acquisition for FY2012 and beyond
Exempts national security vehicle acquisitions from funding limits.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to list tax havens for corporations
Increases penalties on corporations for tax-related offenses involving foreign assets.
Document 3: Amends tax code regarding higher education tax benefits
Disallows tax credits unless proper identification numbers are provided.

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Measures on Security and Education
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislative actions aimed at enhancing national security and supporting education, particularly for service members and STEM graduates.

Document 1: cooperate fully with the United States in the investigation of terror groups
block funding from private Saudi citizens and entities to offshore terror organizations
Document 2: exempt from direct numerical limitations aliens who have earned a Ph.D. degree in science, technology, engineering, or mathematics
H-1B visa numerical limitations aliens who have a U.S. STEM Ph.D.
Document 3: no interest shall accrue on student loans under the Federal Direct Loan program for individuals who are serving on active duty
limits receipt of such benefit to 60 months

topic.name: Climate Change and Disaster Mitigation
topic.description: Legislation focused on addressing climate change impacts through greenhouse gas regulation and disaster risk reduction measures.

Document 1: Requires the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to establish a National Greenhouse Gas Database
Establishes a program for the market-driven reduction of GHGs by covered entities through the use of tradeable emissions allowances.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Army to give funding priority to authorized projects and studies with a primary purpose of hurricane and storm damage risk reduction
Provides for expedited consideration of previously authorized water resources projects that meet such criteria.
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of the Army to review operation of such reservoir, including the water control manual and rule curves, using improved weather forecasts

Requires that if the Secretary determines from results of the review that using improved weather and run-off forecasting methods improves one or more core functions of the Corps at a reservoir, the Secretary shall incorporate such changes in its operation.

topic.name: Federal Legislation on Safety and Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses federal laws aimed at enhancing safety protocols and management practices across various sectors, including aviation, public spaces, and heritage tourism.

Document 1: Amends the federal criminal code to revise the prohibition against entering restricted federal buildings or grounds
Defines 'restricted buildings or grounds' as a posted, cordoned off, or otherwise restricted area
Document 2: Establishes the Journey Through Hallowed Ground National Heritage Area
Requires the Partnership to develop a management plan for the Area that presents strategies and recommendations for promoting tourism
Document 3: Requires a pilot of an aircraft providing emergency medical services to comply with federal safety operating requirements
Requires the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to initiate a rulemaking to establish a standardized checklist of risk evaluation factors

topic.name: Federal Legislative Acts
topic.description: A collection of legislative acts focused on various aspects of federal governance and social welfare.

Document 1: Reinstates provisions of the Defense Base Closure and Realignment Act of 1990
transfer surplus real and personal property at closed or realigned military installations
Document 2: Deems the Federal Housing Finance Agency (FHFA) a designated federal entity
Establishes in the FHFA the Office of the Inspector General
Document 3: Amends part E of title IV of the Social Security Act
requires state child protection standards to ensure that the immigration status alone of a parent shall not disqualify the parent from being a placement for a child

topic.name: Financial Regulation and Lending
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at regulating financial institutions and enhancing lending practices.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Credit Union Act to prohibit an insured credit union from making any member business loan that would result in the total amount of such loans outstanding at that credit union at any one time exceeding either: (1) 1.75 times the actual net worth of the credit union, or (2) 12.25% of the total assets of the credit union.
Directs the Government Accountability Office to study the status of member business lending by insured credit unions.
Document 2: Amends the Home Owners' Loan Act governing federal savings associations to: (1) increase their investment in small business investment companies from 1% to 5%; (2) permit investments in auto loans; (3) repeal the qualified thrift lender requirement for out-of-state branches.
Eliminate the lending limit on small business loans and increase it on other business loans.
Document 3: Directs the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to report to Congress on the effect of its clarification on fair value accounting.
Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act and the Federal Credit Union Act to increase from $100,000 to $250,000 the standard maximum deposit and share insurance amount.

topic.name: Tax Credit and Assistance Programs
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing tax credits and assistance programs for families and education.

Document 1: increase to $7,500 the amount of employer-provided dependent care assistance
exclude from gross income
Document 2: revive in calendar year 2013 the making work pay tax credit
require a reduction of such credit for taxpayers whose modified adjusted gross income exceeds $60,000
Document 3: replaces the Hope Scholarship and Lifetime Learning tax credits
increases the income threshold for reductions in the credit amount based upon modified adjusted gross income

topic.name: Support for Individuals with Neurological Conditions
topic.description: Legislative initiatives aimed at enhancing care and support for individuals living with neurological diseases and their caregivers.

Document 1: comprehensive survey of current adult day programs
develop a set of best practices to help guide the establishment and replication of additional successful adult day programs
Document 3: increase the scholarship program from one to four years
full-time VA health service to up to four years

topic.name: Terrorism Legislation and Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures and policies related to terrorism, including insurance programs, humanitarian relief, and foreign assistance adjustments.

Document 1: Amends the Terrorism Risk Insurance Act of 2002 (TRIA) to extend the Terrorism Insurance Program through December 31, 2020.
Specifies the aggregate industry insured losses resulting from certified acts of terror which will trigger the federal share of compensation under the Program.
Document 2: Provides permanent resident status adjustment for a requesting alien who was on September 10, 2001, the wife, child, or dependent son or daughter of a lawful nonimmigrant alien who died as a result of the September 11, 2001, terrorist attacks.
Makes the provisions of this Act inapplicable to an alien who is inadmissible or deportable under criminal or security grounds, including September 11, 2001, terrorist activity.
Document 3: Amends the Palestinian Anti-Terrorism Act of 2006 to revise the presidential certification and related provisions required in order for U.S. assistance to be provided to the Palestinian Authority.

topic.name: Environmental Regulation and Management
topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of environmental regulation, including waste management, water conservation, and preventing regulatory capture.

Document 1: Amends the Solid Waste Disposal Act, with respect to the regulation of wastes associated with the exploration, development, or production of crude oil, natural gas, or geothermal energy
promulgate regulations regarding standards applicable to generators, transporters, and owners and operators of facilities for the treatment, storage, or disposal of wastes described under this Act
Document 2: comprehensive study of the water management, needs, and conservation of the Apalachicola-Chattahoochee-Flint River System
recommendations for an approach to determine water limits that recognize the needs of all water users along the System
Document 3: Establishes the Office of Regulatory Integrity in the Office of Management and Budget (OMB)
investigate and report on the influence of concentrated economic interests on federal agencies that results in agency action or inaction that fails to advance the mission of the agency

topic.name: Health and Environmental Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses regulations and initiatives aimed at managing health risks and environmental impacts related to greenhouse gases, mental health crises, and antibiotic-resistant infections.

Document 1: Requires the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to determine the quantity of each greenhouse gas that makes the same contribution to global warming as one metric ton of carbon dioxide.
Directs each covered entity to report to the Administrator its greenhouse gas emissions for the prior calendar year.
Document 2: This bill requires the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration to award grants to states for real-time, Internet-based bed registries.
Facilitate the identification of space for the temporary treatment of an individual in psychiatric or substance abuse crisis.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to promulgate regulations relating to MRSA (methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus).
Requires each acute care hospital to screen each patient entering an intensive care unit or other high-risk hospital department.

topic.name: Consumer Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents addresses various aspects of consumer protection laws and regulations across different sectors.

Document 1: prohibits a sanctuary jurisdiction from receiving grants
increases prison term for illegal reentry
Document 2: prohibit a creditor from extending a mortgage without appraisal
establishes civil monetary penalties for unfair appraisal practices
Document 3: requires disclosure of fees and values in coin sales
treats violations as unfair or deceptive acts

topic.name: Concussion Management in Education
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving concussion management and prevention in educational settings.

Document 1: Protecting Student Athletes from Concussions Act of 2015
Each local educational agency must develop and implement a standard plan for concussion safety and management for its public schools.
Document 3: National Traumatic Brain Injury Research and Treatment Improvement Act of 2015

Requires the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) to evaluate existing surveillance and data collection systems that track the incidence and circumstances of traumatic brain injury, including concussion.

topic.name: LGBT Rights and Federal Oversight
topic.description: This cluster of documents addresses the promotion and protection of LGBT rights within the framework of federal oversight and management.

Document 1: establish in the Department of State's Bureau of Democracy, Human Rights, and Labor a Special Envoy for the Human Rights of LGBT Peoples
develop or update annually for five years a U.S. global strategy to prevent and respond to discrimination and violence against LGBT people.
Document 2: strengthen the management and administration of agency activities and coordination among local federal officers
provide a forum for the exchange of information relating to programs and management methods and problems between the national headquarters of agencies and the field.
Document 3: requires a report to Congress listing required DOD reports that interfere with DOD's capacity to achieve an audit of its financial statements
ensure financial accountability in such programs.

topic.name: Health Insurance Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on regulating health insurance practices and ensuring coverage for individuals.

Document 1: Amends the McCarran-Ferguson Act to provide that nothing in that Act shall modify, impair, or supersede the operation of any of the antitrust laws with respect to the business of health insurance.
Applies prohibitions against using unfair methods of competition to the business of health insurance.
Document 2: Prohibits any of the 12 months beginning with the first month of an applicable individual's loss of health insurance coverage from being taken into account in applying the Internal Revenue Code requirement to maintain minimum essential coverage.
Defines 'applicable individual' as an individual who has coverage under an employer-sponsored health plan or individual health insurance coverage.
Document 3: Amends the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act to extend to U.S. territories a program requiring the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish a temporary high risk health insurance pool program.
Provides health insurance coverage to eligible individuals with a preexisting condition.

topic.name: Veterans Assistance Programs
topic.description: Documents related to the support and benefits provided to veterans and their families through various programs and acts.

Document 1: establish in the Department of Defense (DOD) the Office of Technical Assistance
provide such person necessary information to apply for any benefits
Document 2: distribution of information to members of the Armed Forces, veterans, and their dependents
assist in the preparation and presentation of veterans' benefits claims

topic.name: Government Oversight and Accountability
topic.description: Legislation aimed at ensuring government agencies uphold constitutional rights and maintain accountability in their operations.

Document 1: Prohibits the IRS from implementing or enforcing any requirement of the PPACA until certification regarding taxpayer rights is provided.
Requires certification to be submitted to Congress.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure to authorize the waiver of court fees if the interest of justice justifies such a waiver.
States that the interest of justice includes the establishment of constitutional or other important precedent.
Document 3: Prohibits the executive branch from exempting or deferring removal of unlawfully present aliens without constitutional authority.
Declares that any executive branch action intending to circumvent the objectives of this Act shall be null and void.

topic.name: Government Policy and Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating various sectors, including education, healthcare, and energy efficiency.

Document 1: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965
Prohibits the Administrator of the Transportation Security Administration (TSA) from implementing the Screening of Passengers by Observation Techniques (SPOT) program.
Document 2: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
Restores the option under previous law to elect to change from a MedicareAdvantage to the original Medicare fee-for-service plan.
Document 3: Amends the Public Utility Regulatory Policies Act of 1978 (PURPA)
Requires each retail electric supplier to submit to the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) a quantity of federal renewable electricity credits.

topic.name: Maritime and Fisheries Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses laws and amendments related to maritime operations, fishing permits, and the protection of marine mammals within the context of federal and state regulations.

Document 3: Amends the Natural Gas Act to repeal the exclusive authority of the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) to approve or deny an application for the siting, construction, expansion, and operation of liquefied natural gas (LNG) terminals.
Predicates FERC approval or denial authority regarding such application upon the express concurrence of each state affected by the application.

topic.name: Courtroom Media Coverage Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts to regulate and permit media coverage of court proceedings while balancing the rights of involved parties.

Document 1: Requires the Supreme Court to permit television coverage of all open sessions of the Court unless it decides by majority vote that allowing such coverage in a particular case would violate the due process rights of any of the parties involved.
Document 3: Authorizes the presiding judge of a U.S. appellate court (including the Supreme Court) or U.S. district court to permit the photographing, electronic recording, broadcasting, or televising to the public of court proceedings over which that judge presides, except when such action would constitute a violation of the due process rights of any party.

topic.name: Federal Legislation for Public Safety and Environmental Protection
topic.description: This topic encompasses federal legislative acts aimed at enhancing public safety, environmental protection, and management of natural resources.

Document 1: Modifies the project for flood damage reduction, American and Sacramento Rivers, California
authorize improvements to flood damage reduction facilities
Document 2: Assigns responsibility to the Federal Protective Service for the protection of federal buildings
develop safety and security standards and best practices
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to enter into an agreement for the management of free-roaming wild horses
