provide for cost-effective management of such horses

topic.name: Privacy and Security Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the protection of personal information and the enhancement of penalties for violations related to privacy and security.

Document 1: prohibit intentionally: (1) intercepting geolocation information pertaining to another person;
sets penalties for violations.
Document 2: ensure that every primary care health professional employed full-time for such agencies have continuing education courses relating to nutrition.
report to Congress annually on the extent to which it has adopted and encouraged such guidelines.
Document 3: increase the maximum fine for economic espionage (i.e., stealing or obtaining, duplicating or conveying, or buying or possessing trade secrets without authorization
ensure reasonable consistency with other relevant directives, guidelines and statements, and related federal statutes.

topic.name: Environmental and Energy Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at regulating emissions, promoting energy efficiency, and enhancing public health through wellness programs.

Document 1: Requires the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to distribute emission allowances annually
Defines 'carbon leakage' to mean any substantial increase in GHG emissions by manufacturing facilities located in countries without commensurate GHG regulation
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to expand the tax credit for investment in energy property
Qualified high-efficiency linear generator property is a stationary linear generator power plant which has a nameplate capacity of less than 2,000 kilowatts
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to establish standards for programs of health promotion and disease prevention
Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to establish a 10-state demonstration project to apply wellness program standards

topic.name: Education and Infrastructure Funding
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts that provide funding and support for educational systems and infrastructure development.

Document 1: Amends title II of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 (ESEA) to create a new part E
Directs the Secretary of Education to allot funds to states for subgrants to local educational agencies (LEAs)
Document 2: Directs the Administrator of the Maritime Administration to make grants to states or port authorities
cover the direct or indirect costs for repair or construction of commercial strategic seaports

topic.name: Energy and Infrastructure Resilience
topic.description: The documents focus on enhancing energy efficiency, promoting clean energy technologies, and improving the resilience of water infrastructure in response to environmental changes.

Document 1: competitive matching grants to institutions for energy research
development of low-carbon clean and green energy
Document 2: require CBO analysis to estimate job creation in energy and infrastructure
regional and state-level estimates of jobs
Document 3: establish the Water Infrastructure Resiliency and Sustainability Program
increase the resiliency or adaptability of water systems

topic.name: Legislative Regulatory Reforms
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at reforming regulatory frameworks across different sectors, including education, healthcare, and notarization.

Document 1: Interstate Recognition of Notarizations Act of 2010
Requires each federal and state court to recognize any lawful notarization occurring in or affecting interstate commerce.
Document 2: Chiropractic Health Parity for Military Beneficiaries Act
Directs the Secretary of Defense (DOD) to complete development of a plan to provide chiropractic health care services.

Document 3: College Cost Reduction Act of 2012
Directs the Secretary of Education to establish the Higher Education Regulatory Reform Task Force.

topic.name: Federal Land and Travel Policies
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management, access, and benefits associated with federal lands and travel for military personnel and rural communities.

Document 1: expands the authority of the Secretary of Defense (DOD) to accept travel benefits
providing humanitarian support to members and eligible beneficiaries
Document 2: directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to cease all implementation and enforcement of the travel management rule
requires the Secretary to consult with affected counties for the purpose of incorporating their needs
Document 3: authorizes the Departments of the Interior and Agriculture to offer for competitive sale by auction 8% of the federal land
requires proceeds to be deposited into the Highway Trust Fund

topic.name: Education Funding and Taxation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the intersection of education funding, tax credits, and affordability of educational resources.

Document 1: requires the Secretary of the Treasury to make an annual determination of states that have chosen to opt-out of K-12 education grant programs
allows individual taxpayers in states that opt-out a refundable tax credit
Document 2: requires the Department of the Treasury to report on actions necessary to offer pre-prepared tax returns
expresses the sense of Congress that the IRS should begin offering pre-prepared tax returns to taxpayers
Document 3: requires publishers to inform teachers about textbook prices and availability
directs federally-assisted institutions of higher education to include textbook prices on course schedules

topic.name: Land Management and Conservation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the management, conservation, and recreational use of public lands and natural resources.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to convey...
Requires the fair market value of the National Forest System land to be conveyed under this Act to be determined based on an appraisal...
Document 2: Designates certain land under the jurisdiction of the Taos Field Office...
Withdraws the lands identified as 'Land Withdrawn From Mineral Entry'...
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the department in which the Coast Guard is operating to work through the International Maritime Organization...
Promote safe commercial maritime navigation by means of icebreaking when necessary.
